methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Robotics & Manufacturing (found 0 same-stage, 2 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 7 same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

private.name: Formic
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series A)

data.narrative:
SendCutSend is a Series A-stage robotics and manufacturing company that has raised approximately $110 million to date. The valuation analysis draws from a comp universe of nine peers: one publicly traded entity (277810.KQ) and eight private companies including Divergent, Exotec, Locus Robotics, Bedrock Robotics, and Dexterity.

The peer-median method proved inapplicable due to insufficient data density within narrowly defined filters. Of seven same-sector robotics and manufacturing peers identified, none satisfied the minimum threshold of three comparable datapoints when filtered by stage and valuation tier. Applying the sector median without tighter controls would introduce material distortion given the heterogeneity of robotics subsectors and deployment models across the peer set.

The insufficient comparable depth means valuation must rely on alternative approaches not detailed in this dataset. Key uncertainties center on unit economics, path to profitability, and the degree to which SendCutSend's particular robotics application commands a premium or discount relative to the broader cohort. The public market reference (277810.KQ) and private peer valuations provide directional context but lack the granularity needed to triangulate a precise range. Resolution requires expansion of comparable data or reliance on discounted cash flow or revenue multiple methods calibrated to company-specific fundamentals.
